name: Dev Guild
summary: The engineering arm of Greenpill Network, building open-source coordination tools for chapters, funders, and regenerative communities.
description: The Dev Guild ships public tooling for impact measurement, capital allocation, community governance, reputation, and public network surfaces. Public sources connect the guild to Green Goods, GreenWill, Impact Reef, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Octant funding, Gitcoin rounds, and recurring builder programming.
oneliner: Building open-source coordination tools for the regenerative network.

outputs.label: Impact verification
outputs.summary: Green Goods and related tools help communities document and verify regenerative work.

outputs.label: Capital allocation
outputs.summary: Grant Ships, Cookie Jar, and related experiments explore public-goods funding mechanisms.

outputs.label: Reputation primitives
outputs.summary: GreenWill explores public-good reputation and pay-it-forward activity.

outputs.label: Public writing
outputs.summary: The Dev Guild Paragraph documents builder work and grant-facing updates.

mandateParagraphs: The Dev Guild is the engineering arm of Greenpill Network. It designs and ships open-source software that helps chapters and allied communities coordinate without relying on private intermediaries.
The strongest public proof points are Green Goods, GreenWill, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Impact Reef, public GitHub repositories, and the Octant / Gitcoin funding trail.

Where existing tools already do the job, the public direction should frame the guild as an integrator and steward of coordination infrastructure rather than a team rebuilding every primitive from scratch.

cadence.summary: Public sources confirm recurring builder programming, but exact call cadence should be steward-confirmed before launch copy promises a standing schedule.
cadence.format: Builder spaces and public async work

principles.title: Public by default
principles.body: Use public repositories, public docs, and public grant updates whenever they can carry the claim.

principles.title: Integrate before rebuilding
principles.body: Frame the guild around practical coordination infrastructure and integrations with tools such as EAS, Gitcoin, Allo, KarmaGAP, and Green Goods.

principles.title: Chapter-informed tooling
principles.body: Show how tools support local chapters and real regenerative work, not software for its own sake.
